remember: Students will be able to recall the types of not-for-profit organisations and their characteristics.
Students will be able to identify the financial statements prepared by not-for-profit organisations.
Students will be able to list the important features of a not-for-profit organisation's financial statements.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of accounting for not-for-profit organisations.
Students will be able to explain the preparation of not-for-profit organisations' financial statements.
Students will be able to interpret the financial statements of not-for-profit organisations.
apply: Students will be able to prepare the financial statements of a not-for-profit organisation.
Students will be able to apply accounting standards to the financial statements of a not-for-profit organisation.
Students will be able to prepare a budget for a not-for-profit organisation.
analyze: Students will be able to analyse the financial performance of a not-for-profit organisation.
Students will be able to compare the financial performance of different not-for-profit organisations.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of a not-for-profit organisation's financial management.

ncert_objectives: Explain the significance of accounting for not-for-profit organisations.
Describe the main features of a not-for-profit organisation's financial statements.
Explain the preparation of income and expenditure account and balance sheet of a not-for-profit organisation.
Explain the preparation of a fund account of a not-for-profit organisation.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to prepare the financial statements of a not-for-profit organisation as per the given scenario.
Students will be able to apply the accounting standards to the financial statements of a not-for-profit organisation.
Students will be able to identify and explain the key items in the financial statements of a not-for-profit organisation.
